frame_analyses.description: Needle being loaded onto the needle holder.
Initial suture placement through the marked points on the drain.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Suture placement appears slightly off from the optimal entry points.

frame_analyses.description: First throw being executed with a surgeon's knot (double throw).
Second throw being performed with alternate hand usage.
Third throw being completed with another hand switch.
Cutting of the suture ends to complete the task.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Suture ends are cut optimally without excess length.

knot_assessments.notes: First throw is a double throw and appears secure.
Second throw is completed with hand switching.

drain_assessment.assessment_note: No visible gap, and the slit is fully closed.

technique_summary: The trainee completed the suture placement and knot tying task successfully, with minimal deviation from marks and no visible gaps in the slit. Knot security and hand switching were executed properly.
improvement_suggestions: Refine suture placement accuracy to reduce deviation.
Maintain consistent hand movements during knot tying.
